+---
+assignees:
+- claude-code
+position_column: todo
+position_ordinal: ab80
+project: spatial-nav
+title: 'Adopt unified spatial-nav policy: edge drill-out + zone-to-zone — supersedes per-direction tactical cards'
+---
+## What
+
+The user has reported four spatial-nav bugs since the per-component cards were closed. They are all symptoms of the same architectural gap: **the kernel has no unified policy for what happens when navigation in a direction has no in-beam candidate at the focused entry's level.** The per-direction tactical cards each tried to fix their symptom in isolation; this card replaces all of them with one rule.
+
+### The policy (per user, 2026-04-27)
+
+**Edge drill-out + zone-to-zone traversal.**
+
+When `BeamNavStrategy::next` runs from a focused entry and finds no in-direction candidate at the entry's level, it walks up the parent chain until either a candidate exists at some ancestor's level or the layer root is reached. In one rule:
+
+1. Score the focused entry's same-level peers (siblings sharing `parent_zone`) for the requested direction. If a candidate satisfies the beam test or scores reasonably, return it.
+2. Otherwise, **escalate**: treat the focused entry's parent zone as the new search origin. Score the parent's same-level peers. Repeat.
+3. If escalation reaches the layer root with no candidate, return `None` (genuine no-op at the layer boundary).
+
+This collapses three things that are separate today:
+- **Leaf rule 1** (in-zone leaves) → the first level of the cascade.
+- **Leaf rule 2** (cross-zone leaf fallback in same layer) → goes away. Cross-column nav happens via escalation: a card with no leaf to its right escalates to its column zone, finds the next column to the right, then the navigator drills the user back into that column's first card.
+- **Zone-only `navigate_zone`** → also goes away. Zones use the same cascade.
+
+### The user trajectories the policy must satisfy
+
+These three trajectories are the regression spec — each must work and each was reported broken at least once:
+
+**Trajectory A — vertical traversal up the entity stack.** From `task:T1` (first card in leftmost column), pressing Up:
+1. Up → `column:TODO.name` (the column header leaf, geographically above T1).
+2. Up → `column:TODO` zone itself (no leaf above the header within the column → escalate to the column zone, which becomes the focused entry).
+3. Up → `ui:perspective-bar` (no zone above the column inside `ui:board` → escalate to `ui:board`'s level, find perspective bar above).
+4. Up → `ui:navbar` (one more escalation if needed; navbar sits above perspective bar at the layer root).
+
+**Trajectory B — cross-column horizontal.** From `task:T1A` (a card), pressing Right:
+- No card to the right inside `column:TODO` → escalate to `column:TODO` → look right at column-zone level → find `column:DOING` → kernel returns `column:DOING`'s moniker → adapter resolves to "drill into column:DOING's last-focused leaf, or its first card." (The "drill back in" step is an adapter-level concern, NOT a kernel concern. Kernel returns the zone moniker; the adapter handles the drill-in.)
+
+**Trajectory C — left from leftmost column / up from board.** From any leaf in the leftmost column, pressing Left:
+- No leaf to the left → escalate to column → no zone to the left of `column:TODO` → escalate to `ui:board` → no zone to the left of `ui:board` → return `None`. Genuine no-op (the user said Left here is fine to no-op; their fix is via Up).
+
+From any zone or leaf inside `ui:board`, pressing Up enough times eventually reaches `ui:perspective-bar` → `ui:navbar` → no-op above the layer root.
+
+### Files in scope
+
+**Kernel (the actual fix):**
+
+- `swissarmyhammer-focus/src/navigate.rs` — rewrite `BeamNavStrategy::next` so a single cascade replaces `navigate_leaf` + `navigate_zone` + the various beam helpers. Pseudo-code:
+
+  ```rust
+  fn next(&self, registry, focused, direction) -> Option<Moniker> {
+      let mut current = registry.entry(focused)?;
+      loop {
+          // Score same-level peers (siblings with matching parent_zone, same kind).
+          if let Some(target) = beam_among_siblings(registry, current, direction) {
+              return Some(target);
+          }
+          // Escalate to parent zone, if any.
+          let Some(parent_key) = current.parent_zone() else { return None; };
+          let Some(parent) = registry.entry(&parent_key) else { return None; };
+          current = parent;
+      }
+  }
+  ```
+
+  Edge commands (`First`, `Last`, `RowStart`, `RowEnd`) keep their current at-the-focused-level behavior — they are deliberate jumps within a level, not edge-cascading.
+
+  Override (rule 0) still runs first.
+
+- `swissarmyhammer-focus/src/state.rs` — when `navigate_with` receives a zone moniker as the result, the adapter is responsible for "drill back in" semantics if the user expected to land on a leaf. Today `navigate_with` just calls `focus(target_key)`; that already focuses the zone. If the policy is "land on the zone, let next press drill in," then no change. **Decision needed:** does the kernel ever pre-drill-in, or does the user's next press do it explicitly? Default: kernel never auto-drills-in; the user pressing again from the zone walks deeper. This matches the strict "navigation moves one step at a time" contract.
+
+**React side (no behavior change, but tests assert the new policy):**
+
+- No primitive code changes expected. `<FocusScope>`, `<FocusZone>`, `<Focusable>` continue to register the same way.
+
+**Tests — kernel is authoritative, browser test is supplementary:**
+
+- `swissarmyhammer-focus/tests/navigate.rs` — add three integration tests that build the realistic board shape (layer "window" → ui:navbar zone with leaves, ui:perspective-bar zone with leaves, ui:board zone → column zones → cards + column.name leaf) and assert the three user trajectories A, B, C. **The kernel test is the definitive test for the algorithm. If it passes, the algorithm is right.**
+
+- `kanban-app/ui/src/components/board-view.unified-nav.spatial.test.tsx` (new) — browser-mode test that captures `spatial_register_*` calls into a JS shadow registry, runs the new cascade against the captured graph, and asserts the React side emits `focus-changed` and renders the expected `data-focused` attribute. **This test verifies wiring, not algorithm.** When the kernel test passes but this test fails, the bug is in the React layer's registration shape, not in the algorithm.
+
+### What this card replaces
+
+These four cards are **superseded** — close them as "subsumed by [this card's ULID]" once this card is in flight, **except** keep their existing test files as regression guards (move them under this card's test list):
+
+- `01KQ7GWE9V2XKWYAQ0HCPDE0EZ` (cross-column right) — currently `doing`. Coordinate with the implementer: either fold their in-flight work into this card's kernel rewrite, or let the tactical fix ship and re-target the kernel under this card.
+- `01KQ7RR4MJJPVTTB7GS6RKRH9E` (vertical card nav) — `todo`, fully subsumed.
+- `01KQ7PXYP62VQ18K9XYS4Y42GA` (end-to-end smoke) — its Family 2 test ("right from card lands on card in column B") needs to assert observable outcome only, NOT mechanism. Update it to assert "focus lands on a card in column B" without mentioning rule 2.
+- The "left from leftmost column → navbar" gesture I was about to file separately is folded into this card.
+
+### Internal contradictions this card resolves
+
+(Audit findings from the prior conversation.)
+
+1. Two cards diagnosed the same architectural gap as four-six "suspect root causes" each. The unified policy makes the suspect lists moot: the answer is "implement the cascade." No more speculation.
+2. Cross-column right vs. left-to-navbar were two different mechanisms today (rule-2 leaf fallback vs. drill-out). Under this policy, both become one mechanism (escalation cascade).
+3. Tests were asserting on kernel mechanism (rule 1, rule 2). They now assert on observable outcome only.
+4. The JS-shadow-registry tests were doing kernel work (running their own port of `BeamNavStrategy`). The kernel integration test in `swissarmyhammer-focus/tests/navigate.rs` is the source of truth for the algorithm; the browser test only checks wiring. The JS port can drift from Rust without invalidating either test as long as the kernel test pins the algorithm.
+5. The `<Inspectable>` refactor (`01KQ7K7KZNR3EHS9SY0XY79NYE`) and `<Focusable>` deletion (`01KQ5PSMYE3Q60SV8270S6K819`) overlap on the same primitives. They are independent of this card; cross-link them as a note (a follow-up coordination card if needed). Not blocked by this work.
+
+### What this card does NOT do
+
+- Does not change keymap dispatch — `nav.up/down/left/right` continue to call `spatial_navigate`.
+- Does not introduce auto-drill-in (kernel returns the zone moniker; subsequent press inside that zone is a separate user action).
+- Does not change `<FocusZone>` / `<FocusScope>` registration shape.
+- Does not change override rule 0; overrides still run first.
+
+### Open question — answer before merge
+
+**Test fidelity.** The browser-mode tests still mock the Tauri boundary with a JS port of the kernel. That cannot catch Rust-React serialization bugs, real `focus-changed` event timing, or kernel-vs-port drift. The kernel integration test (`cargo test`) is the source of truth for the algorithm. Decision needed: do we accept the browser tests as wiring-only and document the gap, or commit to a real-Tauri test rig as a follow-up? File a follow-up card either way; do NOT block this card on the answer.
+
+## Acceptance Criteria
+
+- [ ] `swissarmyhammer-focus/src/navigate.rs` implements the cascade. `BeamNavStrategy::next` walks the parent chain when no in-direction candidate exists at the focused entry's level, until a candidate is found or the layer root is reached.
+- [ ] `navigate_leaf` and `navigate_zone` are merged into one cascade implementation. The leaf-rule-2 cross-zone leaf fallback is deleted.
+- [ ] Three Rust integration tests in `swissarmyhammer-focus/tests/navigate.rs` pin trajectories A, B, C from the spec above:
+  - `unified_trajectory_a_up_from_first_card_walks_through_column_to_perspective_bar`
+  - `unified_trajectory_b_right_from_card_in_column_a_lands_on_column_b`
+  - `unified_trajectory_c_left_from_leftmost_card_returns_none_at_layer_root`
+- [ ] Browser test `board-view.unified-nav.spatial.test.tsx` captures registrations from the production wiring and asserts the resulting `data-focused="true"` element matches each trajectory's expected target. Asserts only on observable outcome, NOT on which kernel rule fired.
+- [ ] Trajectory A's three Up presses from `task:T1` produce the sequence `task:T1` → `column:TODO.name` → `column:TODO` → `ui:perspective-bar`. Each step is one keystroke, no auto-skipping.
+- [ ] Edge commands (`First`, `Last`, `RowStart`, `RowEnd`) still operate on the focused entry's level only (no escalation cascade for them).
+- [ ] Override rule 0 still runs first and short-circuits the cascade.
+- [ ] Cross-column right (the `01KQ7GWE9V2XKWYAQ0HCPDE0EZ` regression) still works, now via escalation rather than rule 2 leaf fallback. The existing `board-view.cross-column-nav.spatial.test.tsx` still passes — its mechanism comments need updating, but its observable assertions hold.
+- [ ] Vertical card nav (the `01KQ7RR4MJJPVTTB7GS6RKRH9E` symptom) works: Down from `task:T1` → `task:T2`, etc.
+- [ ] `cargo test -p swissarmyhammer-focus -p swissarmyhammer-kanban` is green.
+- [ ] `cd kanban-app/ui && npm test` is green.
+
+## Tests
+
+### Kernel integration tests (authoritative)
+
+`swissarmyhammer-focus/tests/navigate.rs` — three new tests building the realistic shape:
+
+- One `LayerKey` "window" containing:
+  - `ui:navbar` zone (parent_zone = None) at the top of the layout, with `ui:navbar.search`, `ui:navbar.inspect` leaves inside.
+  - `ui:perspective-bar` zone (parent_zone = None) below navbar, with `perspective_tab:default`, `perspective_tab:secondary` leaves.
+  - `ui:board` zone (parent_zone = None) below perspective-bar, with `column:TODO`, `column:DOING`, `column:DONE` zones inside.
+  - Each column zone has a `column.name` leaf at the top and three `task:*` leaves below it.
+
+Test cases:
+
+1. `unified_trajectory_a_up_from_first_card_walks_through_column_to_perspective_bar`
+   - `nav("task:T1", Up) == Some("column:TODO.name")`
+   - `nav("column:TODO.name", Up) == Some("column:TODO")` (the column zone moniker)
+   - `nav("column:TODO", Up) == Some("ui:perspective-bar")`
+   - `nav("ui:perspective-bar", Up) == Some("ui:navbar")`
+   - `nav("ui:navbar", Up) == None`
+
+2. `unified_trajectory_b_right_from_card_in_column_a_lands_on_column_b`
+   - `nav("task:T1A", Right) == Some("column:DOING")` — the kernel returns the zone moniker; the adapter handles drill-in if needed.
+   - Mirror left from `task:T1B`.
+
+3. `unified_trajectory_c_left_from_leftmost_card_returns_none_at_layer_root`
+   - `nav("task:T1A", Left) == None` — escalation reaches `ui:board`, no left sibling at the layer root, return None.
+   - `nav("column:TODO", Down) == None` if no zone below `column:TODO` at the column-tier or board-tier or layer-root. The exact answer depends on whether the layout has anything below the board; for the test fixture it's None.
+
+### Browser test (wiring guard)
+
+`kanban-app/ui/src/components/board-view.unified-nav.spatial.test.tsx` — uses the existing JS-shadow-registry harness (or the extracted helper from `01KQ7PXYP62VQ18K9XYS4Y42GA` if that lands first). Asserts:
+
+1. Pressing Up from `task:T1` emits a `focus-changed` event with `column:TODO.name` as the next moniker, AND the rendered DOM's `data-focused="true"` element is `[data-moniker="column:TODO.name"]`. Three presses walk the trajectory. Each step verified by both the event payload and the DOM.
+2. Cross-column right (Trajectory B) — focus lands on `column:DOING` zone first, then optionally on a card inside it after a follow-up press (depending on the adapter's drill-in policy; assert the policy as decided).
+3. Left at the layer-root edge is a no-op — no `focus-changed` event, `data-focused` does not move.
+4. Existing tests keep passing: `board-view.cross-column-nav.spatial.test.tsx` and any vertical-nav test from `01KQ7RR4MJJPVTTB7GS6RKRH9E`. Update their docstrings to reference this card's policy.
+
+### How to run
+
+```
+cargo test -p swissarmyhammer-focus
+cd kanban-app/ui && npm test
+```
+
+Both must pass headless.
+
+## Workflow
+
+- Use `/tdd`. Order:
+  1. Write the three Rust kernel integration tests (`unified_trajectory_a/b/c`). Confirm they fail against the current `navigate_leaf` / `navigate_zone` split.
+  2. Rewrite `BeamNavStrategy::next` as the unified cascade. Delete `navigate_leaf`, `navigate_zone`, and the leaf-rule-2 fallback. Confirm A/B/C pass.
+  3. Run `cargo test -p swissarmyhammer-focus` to confirm no other kernel tests regress. Update any tests that asserted on rule-2 mechanism specifically — they must assert on observable trajectory outcome only.
+  4. Write the browser-mode wiring test. Confirm it passes against the new kernel via the shadow-registry harness.
+  5. Update `board-view.cross-column-nav.spatial.test.tsx`, the vertical-nav test (when filed under `01KQ7RR4MJJPVTTB7GS6RKRH9E`), and `01KQ7PXYP62VQ18K9XYS4Y42GA`'s Family 2 to remove mechanism assertions.
+  6. Move the superseded tactical cards to done with a "subsumed by <this ULID>" note; keep their test files as regression guards.

## What

The user has reported four spatial-nav bugs since the per-component cards were closed. They are all symptoms of the same architectural gap: **the kernel has no unified policy for what happens when navigation in a direction has no in-beam candidate at the focused entry's level.** The per-direction tactical cards each tried to fix their symptom in isolation; this card replaces all of them with one rule.

### The policy (per user, 2026-04-27)

**Edge drill-out + zone-to-zone traversal.**

When `BeamNavStrategy::next` runs from a focused entry and finds no in-direction candidate at the entry's level, it walks up the parent chain until either a candidate exists at some ancestor's level or the layer root is reached. In one rule:

1. Score the focused entry's same-level peers (siblings sharing `parent_zone`) for the requested direction. If a candidate satisfies the beam test or scores reasonably, return it.
2. Otherwise, **escalate**: treat the focused entry's parent zone as the new search origin. Score the parent's same-level peers. Repeat.
3. If escalation reaches the layer root with no candidate, return `None` (genuine no-op at the layer boundary).

This collapses three things that are separate today:
- **Leaf rule 1** (in-zone leaves) → the first level of the cascade.
- **Leaf rule 2** (cross-zone leaf fallback in same layer) → goes away. Cross-column nav happens via escalation: a card with no leaf to its right escalates to its column zone, finds the next column to the right, then the navigator drills the user back into that column's first card.
- **Zone-only `navigate_zone`** → also goes away. Zones use the same cascade.

### The user trajectories the policy must satisfy

These three trajectories are the regression spec — each must work and each was reported broken at least once:

**Trajectory A — vertical traversal up the entity stack.** From `task:T1` (first card in leftmost column), pressing Up:
1. Up → `column:TODO.name` (the column header leaf, geographically above T1).
2. Up → `column:TODO` zone itself (no leaf above the header within the column → escalate to the column zone, which becomes the focused entry).
3. Up → `ui:perspective-bar` (no zone above the column inside `ui:board` → escalate to `ui:board`'s level, find perspective bar above).
4. Up → `ui:navbar` (one more escalation if needed; navbar sits above perspective bar at the layer root).

**Trajectory B — cross-column horizontal.** From `task:T1A` (a card), pressing Right:
- No card to the right inside `column:TODO` → escalate to `column:TODO` → look right at column-zone level → find `column:DOING` → kernel returns `column:DOING`'s moniker → adapter resolves to "drill into column:DOING's last-focused leaf, or its first card." (The "drill back in" step is an adapter-level concern, NOT a kernel concern. Kernel returns the zone moniker; the adapter handles the drill-in.)

**Trajectory C — left from leftmost column / up from board.** From any leaf in the leftmost column, pressing Left:
- No leaf to the left → escalate to column → no zone to the left of `column:TODO` → escalate to `ui:board` → no zone to the left of `ui:board` → return `None`. Genuine no-op (the user said Left here is fine to no-op; their fix is via Up).

From any zone or leaf inside `ui:board`, pressing Up enough times eventually reaches `ui:perspective-bar` → `ui:navbar` → no-op above the layer root.

### Files in scope

**Kernel (the actual fix):**

- `swissarmyhammer-focus/src/navigate.rs` — rewrite `BeamNavStrategy::next` so a single cascade replaces `navigate_leaf` + `navigate_zone` + the various beam helpers. Pseudo-code:

  ```rust
  fn next(&self, registry, focused, direction) -> Option<Moniker> {
      let mut current = registry.entry(focused)?;
      loop {
          // Score same-level peers (siblings with matching parent_zone, same kind).
          if let Some(target) = beam_among_siblings(registry, current, direction) {
              return Some(target);
          }
          // Escalate to parent zone, if any.
          let Some(parent_key) = current.parent_zone() else { return None; };
          let Some(parent) = registry.entry(&parent_key) else { return None; };
          current = parent;
      }
  }
  ```

  Edge commands (`First`, `Last`, `RowStart`, `RowEnd`) keep their current at-the-focused-level behavior — they are deliberate jumps within a level, not edge-cascading.

  Override (rule 0) still runs first.

- `swissarmyhammer-focus/src/state.rs` — when `navigate_with` receives a zone moniker as the result, the adapter is responsible for "drill back in" semantics if the user expected to land on a leaf. Today `navigate_with` just calls `focus(target_key)`; that already focuses the zone. If the policy is "land on the zone, let next press drill in," then no change. **Decision needed:** does the kernel ever pre-drill-in, or does the user's next press do it explicitly? Default: kernel never auto-drills-in; the user pressing again from the zone walks deeper. This matches the strict "navigation moves one step at a time" contract.

**React side (no behavior change, but tests assert the new policy):**

- No primitive code changes expected. `<FocusScope>`, `<FocusZone>`, `<Focusable>` continue to register the same way.

**Tests — kernel is authoritative, browser test is supplementary:**

- `swissarmyhammer-focus/tests/navigate.rs` — add three integration tests that build the realistic board shape (layer "window" → ui:navbar zone with leaves, ui:perspective-bar zone with leaves, ui:board zone → column zones → cards + column.name leaf) and assert the three user trajectories A, B, C. **The kernel test is the definitive test for the algorithm. If it passes, the algorithm is right.**

- `kanban-app/ui/src/components/board-view.unified-nav.spatial.test.tsx` (new) — browser-mode test that captures `spatial_register_*` calls into a JS shadow registry, runs the new cascade against the captured graph, and asserts the React side emits `focus-changed` and renders the expected `data-focused` attribute. **This test verifies wiring, not algorithm.** When the kernel test passes but this test fails, the bug is in the React layer's registration shape, not in the algorithm.

### What this card replaces

These four cards are **superseded** — close them as "subsumed by [this card's ULID]" once this card is in flight, **except** keep their existing test files as regression guards (move them under this card's test list):

- `01KQ7GWE9V2XKWYAQ0HCPDE0EZ` (cross-column right) — currently `doing`. Coordinate with the implementer: either fold their in-flight work into this card's kernel rewrite, or let the tactical fix ship and re-target the kernel under this card.
- `01KQ7RR4MJJPVTTB7GS6RKRH9E` (vertical card nav) — `todo`, fully subsumed.
- `01KQ7PXYP62VQ18K9XYS4Y42GA` (end-to-end smoke) — its Family 2 test ("right from card lands on card in column B") needs to assert observable outcome only, NOT mechanism. Update it to assert "focus lands on a card in column B" without mentioning rule 2.
- The "left from leftmost column → navbar" gesture I was about to file separately is folded into this card.

### Internal contradictions this card resolves

(Audit findings from the prior conversation.)

1. Two cards diagnosed the same architectural gap as four-six "suspect root causes" each. The unified policy makes the suspect lists moot: the answer is "implement the cascade." No more speculation.
2. Cross-column right vs. left-to-navbar were two different mechanisms today (rule-2 leaf fallback vs. drill-out). Under this policy, both become one mechanism (escalation cascade).
3. Tests were asserting on kernel mechanism (rule 1, rule 2). They now assert on observable outcome only.
4. The JS-shadow-registry tests were doing kernel work (running their own port of `BeamNavStrategy`). The kernel integration test in `swissarmyhammer-focus/tests/navigate.rs` is the source of truth for the algorithm; the browser test only checks wiring. The JS port can drift from Rust without invalidating either test as long as the kernel test pins the algorithm.
5. The `<Inspectable>` refactor (`01KQ7K7KZNR3EHS9SY0XY79NYE`) and `<Focusable>` deletion (`01KQ5PSMYE3Q60SV8270S6K819`) overlap on the same primitives. They are independent of this card; cross-link them as a note (a follow-up coordination card if needed). Not blocked by this work.

### What this card does NOT do

- Does not change keymap dispatch — `nav.up/down/left/right` continue to call `spatial_navigate`.
- Does not introduce auto-drill-in (kernel returns the zone moniker; subsequent press inside that zone is a separate user action).
- Does not change `<FocusZone>` / `<FocusScope>` registration shape.
- Does not change override rule 0; overrides still run first.

### Open question — answer before merge

**Test fidelity.** The browser-mode tests still mock the Tauri boundary with a JS port of the kernel. That cannot catch Rust-React serialization bugs, real `focus-changed` event timing, or kernel-vs-port drift. The kernel integration test (`cargo test`) is the source of truth for the algorithm. Decision needed: do we accept the browser tests as wiring-only and document the gap, or commit to a real-Tauri test rig as a follow-up? File a follow-up card either way; do NOT block this card on the answer.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `swissarmyhammer-focus/src/navigate.rs` implements the cascade. `BeamNavStrategy::next` walks the parent chain when no in-direction candidate exists at the focused entry's level, until a candidate is found or the layer root is reached.

- [ ] Edge commands (`First`, `Last`, `RowStart`, `RowEnd`) still operate on the focused entry's level only (no escalation cascade for them).
- [ ] Override rule 0 still runs first and short-circuits the cascade.
- [ ] Cross-column right (the `01KQ7GWE9V2XKWYAQ0HCPDE0EZ` regression) still works, now via escalation rather than rule 2 leaf fallback. The existing `board-view.cross-column-nav.spatial.test.tsx` still passes — its mechanism comments need updating, but its observable assertions hold.
